prompt: Document the LSASS memory dumping technique used by attackers to steal Windows credentials. I want Markdown format.

assertions.name: has_all_8_sections
assertions.description: Document contains all 8 required section headings: Technique Overview, Attack Steps, Detection Opportunities, Data Sources & Log Fields, Pseudocode Queries, and MITRE ATT&CK Mapping

assertions.name: correct_mitre_id
assertions.description: References the sub-technique T1003.001 specifically, not just the parent T1003

assertions.name: fidelity_ratings_present
assertions.description: At least one detection method is labeled with High, Medium, or Low fidelity

assertions.name: pseudocode_uses_udm_fields
assertions.description: Pseudocode queries use UDM-style field names like event.type, process.name, file.path rather than vendor-specific syntax

assertions.name: windows_datasources_present
assertions.description: Data sources section mentions Sysmon event IDs or Windows Security Event IDs
